object: Brand: Apple ; Model line: iPhone ; Model code or variant: iPhone X-generation with notch (visually consistent with iPhone X / XS / XR / 11 / 11 Pro — back not visible, exact model not determinable from this angle) ; Generation/Year: 2017-2019 era (notch design, pre-iPhone 12, pre-flat-edge) ; Color/Finish: black, glossy front ; Material: aluminum frame visible at edges, glass front (back not visible) ; Visible markings: none readable ; Distinguishing features: top centre notch housing TrueDepth camera array, single horizontal earpiece slot at the top edge, slightly curved 2.5D front glass edge, rounded body corners
problem_visual: Defect: severely shattered front glass ; located at: front display panel — main impact point appears near the upper-middle / centre, with radial cracks fanning out in all directions toward the four edges ; severity: severe ; visible signs: dense spider-web fracture pattern, glass shards along several crack lines especially in the lower-middle area, screen is dark (powered off or unresponsive), no obvious black blotches through the cracks suggesting the LCD/OLED panel itself may still be functional
estimated_skill_level: Intermediate — patient, calm hands, magnetic mat
safety_warnings: Power off the iPhone completely before opening
Disconnect the battery flex BEFORE any other internal work
Wear safety glasses — broken glass can flake off
Work on a clean, lit, non-magnetic surface to track tiny screws

uncertainties.question: Which exact iPhone model is it? (— each model uses a different non-interchangeable display assembly)
uncertainties.purpose: Each notch-era iPhone uses a different display assembly with non-interchangeable connectors and dimensions.
uncertainties.instruction: Flip the phone over and count rear cameras: 1 = iPhone X or XR ; 2 = iPhone XS or 11 ; 3 = iPhone 11 Pro. Or check Settings > General > About on any working device synced with it.

uncertainties.question: OEM display or aftermarket? (— OEM keeps True Tone; aftermarket is ~half the price)
uncertainties.purpose: OEM keeps True Tone and original color fidelity. Aftermarket is about half the price but may lose True Tone after the swap.
uncertainties.instruction: If you don't care about True Tone, aftermarket is the budget choice — just pick a reputable seller to avoid dim or off-color panels.

uncertainties.question: Is the battery older than 2 years or below 80% health? (— swap it while the phone is open)
uncertainties.purpose: Aged batteries can swell and risk puncture during handling. Replacing it at the same time is much easier with the phone already open.
uncertainties.instruction: On a working iPhone: Settings > Battery > Battery Health & Charging.
